_comment: Policy for ArtifactTypeClassifier — decides which chips the wizard treats as 'heavyweight' (always require chip-strip confirmation regardless of LLM confidence; tunnel canaries auto-downgrade to miniApp for safety). Heavyweight = the chip represents a real cost commitment: Unity Editor session (~4 GB workdir, license), native macOS toolchain build, microservice deployment infra. The LLM classifier sometimes hallucinates these from thin signals (e.g. 'donkey kong clone' → unityGame at 0.95 confidence because of 'clone' + 'robot'); we'd rather burn one tap than minutes of generation time. Edit + git push + (relaunch OR refreshMchataisourceCache) ships changes with no Swift rebuild.

_chipReasons._comment: Documentation only — not parsed by Swift. Captures the rationale for each entry so future reviewers can decide whether to add/remove chips.
_chipReasons.unityGame: Unity Editor is ~4 GB on disk + needs a Unity Personal license. Building a 3D game requires the user to have committed to that.
_chipReasons.macOSApp: Native macOS app generation invokes xcodebuild + scaffolds a real Xcode project; user should explicitly choose this over a webSPA / miniApp.
_chipReasons.microservice: Microservice generation creates a new mchatai-source/microservices/<id>/ directory with deployment infra; not what the user wants for a casual ask.
